tool.name: cai
tool.summary: Report deterministic codon adaptation index values against a reference profile

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `cai` computes deterministic CAI-like values for strict coding nucleotide sequences. It derives a reference codon-usage profile from either another coding-sequence input or a normalized profile emitted by `codcopy`, then scores each query record against that reference.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts two local inputs: a coding query input and a reference input. The query input is read through the shared sequence loader and must contain strict in-frame nucleotide coding sequences. The reference input may be another coding-sequence file or a normalized codon-profile TSV produced by `codcopy`.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The result is a stable table report with one row per query record. It includes the record identifier, sense-codon count, optional terminal stop codon, and the computed CAI-like value. Stop codons are excluded from weight derivation and a zero-weight codon in the reference yields CAI `0.0` for the affected query.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema cai`. Rust service coverage exercises CAI scoring directly against committed coding fixtures, and `codcopy` interoperability is also tested by writing a temporary normalized profile and reusing it as the CAI reference input.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The v1 method is intentionally strict. Protein inputs are rejected. Query sequences with non-triplet lengths, ambiguous codons, or internal stop codons fail validation. A single terminal stop codon is allowed and reported separately but excluded from the codon profile.

artifacts.label: Codon-query FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed coding-query FASTA fixture with one preferred-codon example and one rare-codon example.

artifacts.label: Codon-reference FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed coding-reference FASTA fixture used to derive deterministic synonymous codon weights.

examples.title: Score coding query sequences against a coding reference fixture
examples.description: Derives a codon-usage reference from the committed coding-reference fixture and reports CAI-like values for the committed query records.

expected_outputs.label: CAI result rows
expected_outputs.description: A two-row table in which the preferred-codon query scores higher than the rare-codon query.
